MASTER CALENDAR ON AUG-23-2021. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE ISSUED TO JAMES F. LEWIN SET FOR SEP-03-2021 IN DEPARTMENT 206 AT 9:30 AM FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER PURSUANT TO THE NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL AND TRIAL ORDER. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR AUG-23-2021; ATTORNEY ANTHONY DUTRA APPEARED IN COURT FOR PLAINTIFF. ATTORNEY PAUL GRUWELL APPEARED IN COURT FOR DEFENDANT. TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE BY ATTORNEY PETER SPROUL FOR DEFENDANTS THROUGH COURTCALL. NO APPEARANCE FOR DEFENDANT MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC. ATTORNEY JAMES LEWIN IS SANCTIONED IN THE AMOUNT OF $250.00 FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER PURSUANT TO THE NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL AND TRIAL ORDER, AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IS SET ON SEP-03-2021 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. THE COURT ORDERED THIS CASE TO MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN DEPT. 624, JUDGE BRADSTREET. PARTIES WILL SEND AN EMAIL BY 4:30PM TODAY TO DEPARTMENT624@SFTC.ORG TO SET THE MSC. TRIAL IS CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR COURT ON NOV-08-2021 AT 9:30 AM IN 206. PLAINTIFF TO GIVE NOTICE. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: D. LOK; COURT REPORTER: MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by any attorney, any party or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DANA MALLOY DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. First Amended Answer on file. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by any attorney, any party or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT DANA E. MALLOY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE AND DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. All issues raised by the Cross-Complaint belong in Probate Department as is ostensibly admitted by the filing of the probate petition and in opposition. Request to remove the partition action to probate court in opposition to the demurrer is not properly before the Court and no authority is cited for the proposition that a partition action could be brought in Probate Department. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
